what_it_does: Implements a read-only 'lenses' layer over a scanned session JSONL file and its embedding index, producing thematic views such as semantic recall, decision surfacing, unresolved-thread detection, catch-up summaries, topic timelines, directive ledgers, compaction-drift detection, and fork-point scoring.
observations: Defines eight lens functions: find, decisions, open_loops, catch_up, timeline, directives, drift, spin_up_points.
Relies on runtime/session_scan for structural rows and runtime/session_recall for semantic embedding search.
Uses module-level regex cue vocabularies for blockers, gaps/tags, decisions, resolutions, Tim questions, and UI language.
decisions() expands the query into decision-phrased variants, merges by max cosine, and adds a small additive bonus for decision cues while keeping rerank off.
open_loops() flags turns containing blocker/gap/question cues and applies a tight 4-turn resolution heuristic.
catch_up() defaults to the period after the last gap longer than one hour.
drift() compares pre-last-compaction assistant decision chunks against post-compaction chunks via max cosine to find topics that may have been dropped.
spin_up_points() scores candidate Tim-directive fork points across three axes: context-state value, FABLE model usage, and UI/description density.
Provides a small CLI that dispatches lens names and accepts --index-dir and a positional argument.
Imports numpy and session_pointintime.build_timeline lazily inside drift().
standouts: drift() treats compaction as a lossy boundary and uses embedding presence to detect dropped decisions.
spin_up_points() explicitly surfaces high-value context states as evidenced fork candidates.
decisions() uses query expansion plus max-cosine merging rather than a single embedding query.
open_loops() is designed as a 'lost-thread killer' with a deliberately narrow resolution window.
conventions: Regex cue vocabularies are declared as module-level constants with descriptive names.
Lens functions take jsonl_path first, then lens-specific args, plus optional k and index_dir.
Private helpers _turns and _clip centralize genuine-turn extraction and text clipping.
Docstrings include design rationale and declared limitations of heuristics.
A LENSES dict maps CLI names to functions for dispatch.
concerns: drift() contains a confusing `if False else` expression that is dead code.
Multiple hardcoded thresholds and scoring weights (0.55 presence, 0.04 decision bonus, 4-turn window, activity/5000) are not configurable.
open_loops() resolution heuristic may misclassify resolved threads due to its tight window.
directives() filters by a hardcoded INJ_PREFIXES tuple that may be brittle.
spin_up_points() scoring formula is ad hoc and not validated.
No input validation for since timestamp or query strings.
notes: A read-only query layer that turns session scans and embeddings into actionable views for tracking decisions, open threads, drift, and valuable fork points.
questions: What exact schema do session_scan rows and session_recall index entries use?
How is the embedding index built and what are its vector dimensions?
What does session_pointintime.build_timeline return and how are boundaries defined?
What is the intended consumer of spin_up_points output and how are scores calibrated?
How is a 'recovery' self spawned at a pre-drift line in practice?
What does FABLE_MODEL represent and why is it singled out?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file adds an interpretive lens layer over existing scan and recall substrates, exposing the documented read-only views.
intention_signals: To make a scanned conversation session queryable and actionable for a user by surfacing decisions, unresolved items, topic arcs, drift across compactions, and high-value fork points without rebuilding the underlying scan/embed pipeline.
novelty: Embedding-presence drift detection across the last compaction boundary, multi-axis fork-point scoring, and decision-query expansion with max-cosine merging.
contribution: A set of interpretive lens functions and a CLI that consume scan and embedding outputs to produce thematic, read-only session views, along with explicit regex cue vocabularies and declared heuristics.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/session_lens.py implements read-only query lenses over a scanned session JSONL and its embedding index, exposing semantic recall, decision surfacing, open-thread detection, catch-up windows, topic timelines, directive ledgers, compaction-drift detection, and fork-point scoring via regex cues and cosine similarity.
intention_for_embedding: To turn raw session scans and embeddings into actionable, human-readable views that help a user track decisions, unresolved threads, drift across compactions, and valuable context states.

symbols.does: Extracts genuine content turns from a session JSONL using structural attribution and text lookup.
symbols.description: Scans the session, keeps rows attributed to user/assistant/compaction/channel, retrieves their text via session_recall._texts_for_lines, and returns a list of enriched turn dicts with line, timestamp, attribution, model, boundary point, epoch, and text.

symbols.does: Normalizes whitespace and clips a string to a maximum character length.
symbols.description: A small text-formatting helper that collapses whitespace and returns the first n characters of a string, used to keep lens output concise.

symbols.does: General semantic recall over the session embedding index.
symbols.description: Delegates directly to session_recall.recall with the supplied query, k, and optional index directory, returning the hits under the 'find' lens name.

symbols.does: Semantic recall biased toward decision-language turns for a topic.
symbols.description: Embeds several decision-phrased variants of the topic, merges chunks by their maximum cosine across queries, adds a small additive bonus when a decision cue regex matches, and returns the top-k results with reranking disabled.

symbols.does: Detects unresolved threads using blocker, gap, and question cues.
symbols.description: Iterates genuine turns, flags turns matching blocker/gap/Tim-question cues, checks the next four turns for resolution cues or a substantial assistant reply, and returns likely-unresolved items latest-first.

symbols.does: Summarizes substantive turns within a time window.
symbols.description: If no 'since' timestamp is given, starts after the last gap exceeding one hour; then returns the largest assistant turns plus all Tim turns in that window, clipped and chronological.

symbols.does: Returns every semantic mention of a topic sorted by time.
symbols.description: Runs semantic recall, filters hits by a cosine threshold, sorts them chronologically, and returns the topic's arc across the session.

symbols.does: Lists every genuine Tim turn in chronological order.
symbols.description: Uses structural user attribution to exclude inject/tool/compaction turns, drops lines starting with known injection prefixes, clips text, and returns the full slate of Tim directives.

symbols.does: Detects decisions from before the last compaction that are weakly present afterward.
symbols.description: Builds a timeline to find the last compaction boundary, loads the embedding index, compares pre-boundary assistant decision chunks against post-boundary chunks via max cosine, and flags low-presence topics as drift candidates with recovery pointers.

symbols.does: Ranks candidate fork points by the value of the context state they open.
symbols.description: Identifies substantial Tim directives as segment seeds, scores each following segment by decision density, open threads, assistant activity, FABLE model usage, and UI/description density, and returns top candidates across three axes.
